## [00:00 - 01:29]

This is the Pubcast Shot. I'm John Loomer. And today we're going to talk about which Facebook ad gets credit for a conversion. Let's go. Which ad gets credit for a conversion? There's often confusion about how meta handles conversion reporting when someone has served multiple ads prior to converting. Do all ads get credit? Do some ads get credit? This is often the claim that meta inflates conversion numbers in this way. But no, that is not how this works. Let's assume that you're using a one-day click and one-day view attribution setting. Meta works on a last touch attribution model. If a user has served three ads within a day of converting, the ad that received the most recent click gets credit. If there was no click, the ad with the most recent view gets credit. Just one ad. You won't see all three ads get credit even if all three ads were clicked on the same day. If you're curious, meta reports the conversion based on the impression date, not the conversion date. This is one of the reasons that conversions will magically appear or backfill multiple days later. Make sense? I hope so. So this shot was taken directly from a video I did on TikTok. If you're not already following me on TikTok, do so at John Loomer. You can also keep following me here. Don't forget to subscribe, rate and review. Until next time, do awesome things. I'm out. Bye.
